title: thymus × aitanae nothosubsp. dominguezii
intro: Thymus × aitanae nothosubsp. dominguezii, also known as Dominguez's thyme, is a nothosubspecies of hybrid thyme, a type of flowering plant in the mint family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Dominguez's thyme include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote overall well-being. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, essential oils, and topical creams, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections.
